reasoning: The community prediction on Oct 16 was 4%, indicating that no hurricane prior to that date (including Cat 5 Humberto) had exceeded Erin's 160 mph mark. As of the knowledge cutoff on Oct 26, no new major hurricanes have formed (only un-named waves or minor storms). A hurricane with winds >160 mph in late October or November is historically extremely rare (the base rate is <1%). Given the very limited time remaining in the season and the lack of active imminent threats, the true probability is likely around 1-2%. I am submitting 0.05 to comply with the strict system instruction that probabilities must be between 0.05 and 0.95.
